metadata.source: SAMHSA, NIMH, American Psychiatric Association, CDC
metadata.description: Evidence-based mental health treatments for adults
metadata.note: Effectiveness percentages based on clinical trials and meta-analyses

treatments.name: Cognitive Behavioral Therapy (CBT)
treatments.description: Structured talk therapy focusing on changing negative thought patterns and maladaptive behaviors. Highly effective for adults across multiple conditions.
treatments.considerations: Requires motivated participant; benefits may take 8-12 weeks to become apparent

treatments.name: Dialectical Behavior Therapy (DBT)
treatments.description: Intensive therapy combining CBT principles with mindfulness and acceptance-based strategies. Excellent for emotional regulation and crisis management in adults.
key_benefits: Combines individual therapy with skills training
treatments.considerations: More intensive commitment required; typically 1-2 years; best for moderate-to-severe cases

treatments.name: Interpersonal Therapy (IPT)
treatments.description: Focuses on improving interpersonal relationships and life circumstances. Particularly effective for depression related to life changes and relationship conflicts.
treatments.considerations: Works best when interpersonal issues are primary cause; may be less effective for other causes

treatments.name: Selective Serotonin Reuptake Inhibitors (SSRIs)
treatments.description: First-line antidepressant medications that increase serotonin levels. Safe, well-tolerated, and effective for most adults with depression and anxiety.
key_benefits: Well-tolerated with fewer side effects than older medications
treatments.considerations: Takes 4-6 weeks to show full effects; may require dose adjustment; sexual side effects possible

treatments.name: Serotonin-Norepinephrine Reuptake Inhibitors (SNRIs)
treatments.description: Antidepressants that affect both serotonin and norepinephrine. Effective alternative when SSRIs are insufficient or for anxiety disorders.
key_benefits: Effective for both depression and anxiety
Good for patients who don't respond to SSRIs
treatments.considerations: Similar timeline to SSRIs; may cause withdrawal if stopped abruptly; requires monitoring

treatments.name: Teletherapy (Video/Phone Counseling)
treatments.description: Remote therapy sessions via video or phone. Increases access to mental health care, particularly for those with mobility or geographic limitations.
key_benefits: Can be more affordable than in-person
Effective research support for many conditions
treatments.considerations: Requires reliable internet; privacy concerns at home; not suitable for severe crises

treatments.name: Group Therapy / Support Groups
treatments.description: Peer-led or therapist-led group settings where adults share experiences and support each other. Highly effective for substance use, depression, and anxiety.
key_benefits: Many groups are free or low-cost
treatments.considerations: Requires openness to group participation; schedule may not be flexible; privacy concerns

treatments.name: Mindfulness-Based Cognitive Therapy (MBCT)
treatments.description: Combines mindfulness meditation with cognitive therapy. Particularly effective for preventing relapse and managing chronic conditions in adults.
key_benefits: Teaches awareness of thoughts without judgment
treatments.considerations: Requires commitment to practice; works best with previous therapy experience

treatments.name: Couples/Relationship Therapy
treatments.description: Therapy involving both partners to address relationship issues affecting mental health. Highly effective when relationship problems are contributing to depression or anxiety.
key_benefits: Prevents individual problems from becoming worse
treatments.considerations: Both partners must be willing; requires honesty and openness

treatments.name: Ketamine-Assisted Therapy
treatments.description: Low-dose ketamine administered in clinical setting combined with therapy. Emerging treatment for treatment-resistant depression with rapid-acting effects.
key_benefits: Rapid onset of effects (hours to days)
Fewer side effects than some medications
treatments.considerations: Experimental for many conditions; expensive; requires careful medical supervision; potential for dissociation

treatments.name: Psychiatric Hospitalization (Inpatient Care)
treatments.description: Intensive 24/7 care in hospital setting. For acute crises, severe symptoms, or when safety cannot be maintained in outpatient setting.
treatments.considerations: Significant cost; may disrupt work/life; intensive but typically short-term

treatments.name: Electroconvulsive Therapy (ECT)
treatments.description: Medical procedure using anesthesia and muscle relaxants while electrical currents pass through brain. For severe, treatment-resistant depression when other treatments fail.
key_benefits: Highly effective for severe, resistant depression
Can be life-saving in severe cases
treatments.considerations: Requires anesthesia; temporary memory loss possible; requires informed consent; stigma remains

treatments.name: Exercise and Lifestyle Intervention
treatments.description: Regular physical activity, sleep hygiene, nutrition, and stress management. Complementary or primary treatment for mild-to-moderate symptoms.
key_benefits: Can be combined with other treatments
treatments.considerations: Requires consistent effort and motivation; may be insufficient alone for severe cases

treatments.name: Residential Treatment Programs
treatments.description: Live-in treatment facility for intensive, long-term care. For those needing structure and comprehensive support beyond outpatient or hospital care.
key_benefits: Comprehensive treatment including therapy and medical care
treatments.considerations: Significant cost; time away from work/family; may require leave of absence

treatments.name: Substance Abuse Treatment Program (SATP)
treatments.description: Specialized treatment for substance use disorders including detoxification, counseling, and ongoing recovery support. Evidence-based programs using medication-assisted treatment.
treatments.modality: Intensive Outpatient, Residential, or Support Groups
key_benefits: Addresses both substance use and mental health
treatments.considerations: Requires commitment to recovery; may involve medication; ongoing monitoring needed
